parts.text: Before building the scalable version, we did the honest thing: tighten to a spec, then prove it — or defer.
The pain is real and measured. Seventeen million agent-authored pull requests, merging at thirty-three percent against eighty-four for humans.
The spike de-risks the hard part: the same change, gated only on who opened it. Human passes, agent is blocked — the one thing GitHub rulesets cannot express, because author is bypass-only.
But the space is half-taken. GitHub gates branches, not authors — yet Mergify and SonarQube are already circling the gap.
And the buyers haven't shown up. The loudest pain is unpaid maintainers — and GitHub already gave them a free fix.
So the honest call: pivot to the open wedge, ship it as OSS — and defer the paid product until someone proves they'll pay.
mergegate. Validate cheap. Build only what's proven. From deemwar.
